ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Nyonya Otah or Baked Nyonya Otah Salad. Closest defensible FoodOn anchor is fish cake (FOODON_03315595) as otah is a spiced fish paste cake. The salad component is not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish, minced or ground, cooked — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for spiced fish paste cake (otah) component
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for Nyonya Otah or Baked Nyonya Otah Salad. A0FJP (minced/ground cooked fish) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for the otah component. The salad and coconut milk components are not captured.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Baked Nyonya Otah Salad is approximately 150g, comprising one or two pieces of baked otah fish cake served on a bed of fresh salad greens.

nutrient_source.title: Baked Nyonya Otah Salad — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Baked Nyonya Otah Salad (cr_id: G00010732). All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 18g/100g based on the known coconut milk content of Nyonya otah preparations; this estimate is documented in notes as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several vitamins are set to -1 as HPB does not report these for this entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Dishes and Spiced Fish Preparations — Analogous Estimate Based on Low-Carbohydrate Spiced Fish Cake
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Baked Nyonya Otah Salad in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at approximately 35 (low category) based on the analogous profile of low-carbohydrate spiced fish cake preparations with coconut milk. Carbohydrate content is only 5.9g/100g; the high fat content (44.54g/100g) substantially slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption, supporting a low GI estimate. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (35 × 5.9 × 1.5) / 100 = 3.1g, confirming very low GL. This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct GI testing of this dish has not been published.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810mg/100g — well above the 600mg/100g high-concern threshold; potassium at 64mg/100g is insufficient to offset the sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Baked Nyonya Otah Salad due to its critically high sodium content of 810mg/100g, primarily from belacan (shrimp paste); if consumed, restrict to a very small portion (≤50g) and avoid other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.9g/100g) and low sugar (4.69g/100g) are favourable, but very high fat (44.54g/100g) including saturated fat (~18g/100g from coconut milk) may worsen insulin resistance with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Baked Nyonya Otah Salad has a low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content suitable for short-term glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes, but the high saturated fat content from coconut milk warrants portion control (≤100g per serving) to avoid worsening insulin resistance and contributing to excess caloric intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high total fat (44.54g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~18g/100g from coconut milk, combined with high sodium (810mg/100g) and cholesterol (26mg/100g), presents a compound cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Baked Nyonya Otah Salad only occasionally and in small portions (≤80g), as the combination of high saturated fat from coconut milk, high sodium from belacan, and elevated cholesterol collectively raises LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~18g/100g) may promote pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); however, turmeric and lemongrass in the spice blend provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit portion size to ≤80g due to high saturated fat content, while noting that the turmeric (curcumin) and lemongrass in the Nyonya spice blend may provide modest anti-inflammatory benefit; high sodium (810mg/100g) may also worsen RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Baked Nyonya Otah Salad is a Peranakan (Nyonya) Singaporean dish combining baked otah — a fragrant, spiced fish paste cake — with fresh salad greens. Otah (also spelled otak-otak) is made from minced or ground fish blended with coconut milk, lemongrass, turmeric, galangal, chilli, kaffir lime leaves, and belacan (fermented shrimp paste), then wrapped and baked until set. The Nyonya style is distinguished by its richer coconut milk content and more complex spice profile compared to the Malay or Indonesian versions. The baked preparation avoids the advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) associated with deep-frying. The resulting dish has a creamy, aromatic, mildly spicy flavour with a soft, custard-like texture from the fish paste and coconut milk, contrasted by the crisp freshness of the salad greens. Nutritionally, this dish is very high in fat (44.54g/100g), predominantly from coconut milk saturated fat (~18g/100g estimated), with low carbohydrates (5.9g/100g) and low protein (1.32g/100g). Sodium is critically high at 810mg/100g due to belacan. Energy density is high at 430 kcal/100g. The dish is not suitable for hypertension or cardiovascular disease patients due to high sodium and saturated fat. It is conditionally suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management given its low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content, provided portion size is controlled. The anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, lemongrass) may offer modest benefit for inflammatory conditions. Commonly served as a starter or side dish at Singaporean Peranakan restaurants and hawker centres, and enjoyed at lunch or dinner.
common_user_queries: Is otah salad suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id: G00010732, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from known coconut milk composition in Nyonya otah preparations and documented as an estimate. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, documented accordingly. Cultural and culinary facts are well-established for Peranakan cuisine.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (18.0g/100g) is an estimate based on the known coconut milk content of Nyonya otah preparations; HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. This value should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis or updated HPB entry when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for Baked Nyonya Otah Salad are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this record as of 2026. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB for this entry; this is plausible given the minimal vegetable fibre contribution in the otah component and the salad greens not being separately quantified in the HPB record. 4. The input record incorrectly listed primary_cuisine as 'Japanese' and ethnic_tags as ['Japanese', 'Western']; these have been corrected to Peranakan (Nyonya) / Singaporean based on the dish identity. 5. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Baked Nyonya Otah Salad has been published in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2026. 6. Halal status is marked false as belacan (shrimp paste) preparation and sourcing must be verified for halal certification; standard commercial belacan may not be halal-certified.
